title: INT.KMP
shortSummary: Field Service Planner — a fleet scheduling engine tracking coffee-machine maintenance cycles.
fullDescription:
INT.KMP is a field-service planning platform I engineered to keep an entire fleet of coffee machines running flawlessly. I built a genuinely platform-independent operator experience in **Flutter**, delivering a single, polished codebase that runs identically on iOS, Android and the web — so technicians in the field and dispatchers at the desk work from the same fluid interface.

Behind that interface sits an isolated, **self-hosted .NET Core** backend that I designed for autonomy and resilience: maintenance cycles, service intervals and machine telemetry are modelled as first-class domain concepts and persisted in **MongoDB**, whose flexible document model elegantly captures the irregular, evolving shape of real-world service records.

My standout engineering effort is a **bespoke authentication and token-management model** purpose-built for field logistics — handling intermittent connectivity, long-lived technician sessions and secure device-bound refresh flows so that a tablet in a basement boiler room stays trustworthy without a constant connection. The result is a robust, offline-tolerant scheduling engine that turns reactive break-fix work into proactive, cycle-driven maintenance.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.KMP

_Last updated: 2026-05-31_

INT.KMP ("the App") is a field-service planning tool for coffee-machine maintenance. This policy explains what data the App processes and why.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account & technician identity** — name, email, device identifier, authentication tokens.
- **Service records** — machine IDs, maintenance cycles, scheduled and completed visits, location of service sites.
- **Operational telemetry** — app version, crash diagnostics (anonymised).

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

Data is processed solely to schedule, track and document maintenance work (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract) and to keep the platform secure (Art. 6 (1)(f) — legitimate interest).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored on a self-hosted backend in the EU using **MongoDB**. All transport is TLS-encrypted; authentication uses short-lived, device-bound tokens.

## 5. Retention

Service records are retained for the duration of the maintenance contract and deleted within 90 days of contract termination unless legal retention periods apply.

## 6. Your Rights

You may request access, rectification, erasure, restriction and portability of your data at any time — see the GDPR instructions for this project.

## 7. Contact

For any privacy question, write to **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.KMP

Under the **General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR)** you hold the following rights regarding the personal data INT.KMP processes about you.

## How to Exercise Your Rights

1. **Right of access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of all data held about you.
2. **Right to rectification (Art. 16)** — correct inaccurate or incomplete data.
3. **Right to erasure (Art. 17)** — request deletion of your account and service history.
4. **Right to restriction (Art. 18)** — pause processing while a dispute is resolved.
5. **Right to data portability (Art. 20)** — receive your data in a machine-readable JSON export.
6. **Right to object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest.

## Submitting a Request

Send an email to **salih.oez@proton.me** with the subject line `GDPR Request — INT.KMP` and the right(s) you wish to exercise. Requests are answered within **30 days**.

## Right to Lodge a Complaint

You may also complain to your local supervisory authority (in Germany: the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia).

title: INT.Cookling
shortSummary: Digital Cooking Companion — a cross-platform cookbook that turns any recipe link, reel, or video into a structured recipe with multimodal AI.
fullDescription:
INT.Cookling is a premium cooking companion I built to kill the most tedious part of home cooking: typing recipes out by hand. A user shares a link from a YouTube video, an Instagram reel, or any food blog, and the app returns a clean, structured recipe — ingredients, steps, calories, and serving size — ready to cook from. I crafted the whole experience in **Flutter** around **Material 3 Expressive**, with a curated crimson-and-canary palette, fluid micro-animations via **flutter_animate**, and a hands-free immersive cooking mode designed to stay readable from across a messy kitchen island.

The heart of the app is a multimodal extraction engine I architected with the **Strategy pattern**: a manager inspects each URL's signature and routes it to a dedicated `WebStrategy`, `YouTubeStrategy`, or `InstagramStrategy`. For video sources, large reels are buffered and streamed to **Firebase Storage** as temporary `gs://` assets, then analysed frame-by-frame by **Firebase Vertex AI (Gemini)** against a strict JSON output schema — all guarded by client-side retries with exponential backoff to ride out rate limits gracefully.

Behind the UI sits a reactive data layer built on **Cloud Firestore** and **RxDart**. My `CentralDataStore` exposes hot `BehaviorSubject` streams of the user's cookbooks and recipes, eliminating duplicate listeners and redundant reads while keeping everything live across offline and online states. Collaborative cookbooks enforce an `owner`/`editor`/`viewer` permission hierarchy backed by Firestore security rules, and the entire codebase follows **Clean Architecture** with feature-based modules, dependency inversion through GetIt + Injectable, and a pure, framework-free domain layer.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.Cookling

_Last updated: 2026-06-02_

INT.Cookling is a cross-platform cooking companion that imports, organises, and shares recipes. This policy explains what personal data the app processes and why.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account** — email address, display name, and profile photo URL (via Firebase Auth / Google Sign-In).
- **User content** — recipes you create or import, ingredients, steps, source URLs, and cookbook collections.
- **Collaboration** — for shared cookbooks we store collaborators' user IDs and email addresses to display membership and enforce owner/editor/viewer permissions.
- **Push tokens** — Firebase Cloud Messaging (FCM) tokens used to deliver notifications.
- **Diagnostics** — crash reports and usage analytics via Firebase Crashlytics and Firebase Analytics.

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

We process account and content data to provide the core service — importing, storing, and sharing recipes (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract). Diagnostics and crash reporting rest on our legitimate interest in a stable, secure app (Art. 6 (1)(f) GDPR). Optional push notifications are sent on the basis of consent (Art. 6 (1)(a) GDPR).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored in Google Firebase (Cloud Firestore and Firebase Storage) and protected by row-level Firestore security rules enforcing per-user ownership and cookbook sharing tiers. Firebase App Check validates legitimate app instances before they may reach Auth, Firestore, or Vertex AI. Transport is encrypted in transit via TLS.

## 5. Retention

Your recipes and cookbooks are retained until you delete them or close your account. Deleting your account removes your profile and the content you own. Diagnostic data is retained only for the limited windows configured in Firebase Crashlytics and Analytics.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.Cookling

INT.Cookling processes personal data under the GDPR — your account details (email, display name, photo), the recipe content you create or import, and, for shared cookbooks, the email addresses of collaborators you invite.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of the personal data we hold about you.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct an inaccurate email, display name, or profile detail.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — request deletion of your account and the content you own.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — ask us to limit processing while a request is reviewed.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — receive your recipes and account data in a machine-readable format.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest, including diagnostics.

## Shared Cookbooks & Collaborator Data

When you invite someone to a cookbook, their email and user ID are stored to display membership and enforce owner/editor/viewer access. If you are a collaborator and want your email removed from a cookbook you did not create, contact the cookbook owner or email us directly and we will assist.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — INT.Cookling`. We respond within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.

title: DieListe
shortSummary: Public List Sharing Platform — a collaborative web app for creating, sharing, and bot-embedding public lists.
fullDescription:
DieListe is a list-sharing platform I built so anyone can create a list, publish it under a clean `/{username}/{slug}` URL, and share it the moment it's ready. I designed the whole experience around bleeding-edge **Angular 21** with an **Nx** monorepo, **PrimeNG 21** components, and **Tailwind CSS v4**, driving every screen reactively through **Angular Signals** rather than heavyweight state libraries — so the editor, drag-and-drop reordering, likes, and bookmarks all feel instant and stay in sync without ceremony.

Behind the UI I leaned on **Appwrite Cloud** as a serverless backend and engineered a genuine collaboration layer on top of it: a per-document permission model where owners grant `view` or `edit` roles, an invitation flow that notifies invitees, an append-only **audit log** that records every permission change, and a public/private toggle wired directly into Appwrite's document-level ACLs. Lists serialize their items to JSON, slugs are generated and de-duplicated per user, and ownership is enforced both in the client and in the storage permissions themselves.

The platform is deployed entirely as code — **Terraform** provisions **Cloudflare Pages** with separate dev and prod environments, **GitHub Actions** handles CI/CD and runs versioned database migrations on deploy, and runtime config is injected per-environment rather than baked into the bundle. I also designed a clean read API contract and documented a **.NET Discord bot** integration, so any published list can be pulled straight into a Discord server as a formatted embed via a slash command.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — DieListe

_Last updated: 2026-06-02_

DieListe is a web application for creating and publicly sharing lists. This policy explains what personal data the app processes and how it is handled.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account** — email address, username, and display name used to register and sign in.
- **List content** — list titles, descriptions, and items you create. Lists are public by default and viewable by anyone via their share URL.
- **Engagement** — likes and bookmarks (stored as your user ID against a list) and the lists shared with you.
- **Sharing & permissions** — collaborator user IDs and roles (`view`/`edit`), and invitations you send or receive.
- **Audit log** — a record of permission changes (acting user, action, timestamp) for each list.

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

We process this data to provide the core service — account access, list creation, public sharing, and collaboration (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract). Audit logging serves our legitimate interest in security and accountability (Art. 6 (1)(f) GDPR).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored in **Appwrite Cloud**. Access is protected by authenticated sessions and enforced with document-level access-control lists: only the owner and explicitly granted collaborators can read or edit a non-public list. Traffic is served over HTTPS via Cloudflare.

## 5. Retention

List and account data are retained until you delete the relevant list or request account deletion. Deleting a list removes its content; audit entries tied to a list are removed with it.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — DieListe

Your account details, list content, and collaboration data (shares, invitations, likes, bookmarks, and audit entries) are personal data under the GDPR. This page explains your rights and how to exercise them.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of your account data and the lists you own.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct your username, display name, or list content directly in the app.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — delete any list you own, or request full deletion of your account and data.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — ask us to limit processing while a request is being resolved.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — receive your lists in a structured, machine-readable (JSON) format.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest, such as audit logging.

## Sharing & Visibility Controls

Every list has a public/private toggle and a per-collaborator permission setting. Switching a list to private removes public read access immediately; revoking a collaborator removes their `view`/`edit` permission. You can review who has access and the audit log of permission changes from the list editor at any time.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — DieListe`. We respond within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.

title: INT.DriverLog
shortSummary: Mileage Logbook — a cross-platform driver's log that turns business trips into a signed, reimbursement-ready PDF.
fullDescription:
INT.DriverLog is a driver's logbook I built to take the friction out of business-mileage reimbursement. Drivers keep a monthly log, record each trip with its route, purpose and odometer readings, and the app turns the whole month into a polished, signature-bearing PDF that's ready to hand to an employer or tax office. I wrote it once in **Flutter** and it runs natively on **Android** and the **web** from a single codebase, fully localised in **English, German and Turkish**.

Under the hood I leaned on a strict **BLoC / Cubit** architecture, with six state objects scoped deliberately across the app — app-wide auth, locale and theme cubits sitting above per-screen profile, logs and trips cubits. Auth is handled by **Firebase Auth** (email/password plus Google Sign-In, with a deliberate `kIsWeb` branch so the web popup flow and the mobile credential flow never collide), and all data lives in a fully user-scoped **Cloud Firestore** tree (`users/{uid}/logs/{logId}/trips/{id}`) that streams in real time via snapshot listeners. Every collection level is locked down by security rules that deny any cross-user access.

The piece I'm most proud of is the **PDF export pipeline**. I capture the driver's signature on a canvas, store it in **Firebase Storage**, then generate a multi-page reimbursement document with the `pdf` and `printing` packages — fetching **NotoSans** fonts at render time so umlauts and Turkish glyphs render perfectly. Each trip's distance flows through an `effectiveDistance` model that prefers a manual override over the raw odometer delta, and the footer computes the total reimbursement from the per-kilometre rate. It's a small app that does one real-world job end to end, and does it cleanly.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.DriverLog

_Last updated: 2026-06-02_

INT.DriverLog is a driver's logbook app for recording business trips and producing reimbursement documents. This policy explains what personal data the app processes and why.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account** — your email address and authentication identifier (email/password or Google Sign-In).
- **Profile** — full name, vehicle license plate, and banking details (IBAN, BIC, bank name) used to populate reimbursement documents.
- **Signature** — a handwritten signature image you draw, stored as a PNG.
- **Trip data** — dates, trip purposes, route waypoints, odometer readings and computed distances for each log.

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

Data is processed solely to provide the logbook and to generate your reimbursement PDFs (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract). Authentication and account security rest on our legitimate interest (Art. 6 (1)(f) GDPR).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored in **Google Firebase** (Cloud Firestore and Firebase Storage) under your authenticated user ID. Security rules enforce strict per-user isolation: no user can read or write another user's logs, trips, profile or signature. Authentication is handled by Firebase Auth over encrypted connections.

## 5. Retention

Your data is retained while your account exists. Deleting your account or individual logs removes the associated records; the signature image and profile data are deleted on request.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.DriverLog

INT.DriverLog processes personal data under the GDPR — including your name, banking details (IBAN/BIC), vehicle license plate, signature and trip routes. You have the following rights over that data.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of all profile, log and trip data held under your account.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct your name, banking details, license plate or any trip record at any time directly in the app.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — delete individual logs, your signature, or your entire account and its data.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — ask us to limit processing while a dispute is resolved.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — receive your logs and trips in a machine-readable export.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest.

## Banking & Signature Data

Your IBAN, BIC, bank name and handwritten signature are used only to populate the reimbursement PDFs you generate. They are stored under your private, user-scoped Firebase records and are never shared with third parties. You can clear or update them in the profile screen, or have them erased on request.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — INT.DriverLog`. We respond within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.

title: INT.PrintIt
shortSummary: 3D Printing Order Manager — a cross-platform platform that runs an entire fleet of 3D printers from request to finished print.
fullDescription:
INT.PrintIt is a cross-platform app I built to take a 3D print from a rough idea all the way to a finished part, without the usual back-and-forth of spreadsheets and chat messages. A user uploads an **STL**, **OBJ**, **GCODE** or **3MF** file, picks a printer, and the request flows through an explicit order lifecycle — `created → pendingApproval → approved → scheduled → printing → complete` — that every party can see in real time. Printer owners approve or reject jobs, estimate print duration, and slot work onto a conflict-aware calendar, while a built-in real-time chat keeps the conversation attached to the order itself.

The whole experience is one **Flutter** codebase running natively on Android, iOS, and the Web. I architected it as a clean layered design — UI, **Bloc/Cubit** state management, and a repository data layer — sitting on a fully **serverless Firebase** backend: **Cloud Firestore** for real-time sync, **Firebase Storage** for model files, **Firebase Auth** for identity, and **Cloud Messaging** for push. Authentication is genuinely multi-provider, supporting email/password alongside **Google, Apple, and GitHub** OAuth sign-in.

What I'm most proud of is the engineering around trust and timing. A three-tier **role-based access model** (user, printer owner, admin) is enforced both in the app and in deny-by-default **Firestore security rules**, with an `accessGranted` gate so new accounts stay sandboxed until approved. The scheduler runs its own overlap-detection algorithm to prevent double-booking a printer — or to allow controlled overlap behind an explicit approval step — and a **Cloud Function** in `europe-west3` listens on the notifications collection to fan status changes and new messages out as FCM push notifications.
privacyPolicyMarkdown:
# Privacy Policy — INT.PrintIt

_Last updated: 2026-06-02_

INT.PrintIt is a cross-platform app for managing 3D printing orders. This policy explains what personal data the app processes and why.

## 1. Data Controller

Salih-Can Özdemir · Düsseldorf, Germany · salih.oez@proton.me

## 2. Data We Process

- **Account** — email address, display name, chosen authentication provider (email, Google, Apple, or GitHub), assigned role and printer assignments.
- **Orders & files** — order titles, descriptions, and the 3D model files you upload (STL, OBJ, GCODE, 3MF, ZIP) or the download links you provide.
- **Scheduling** — reservation start/end times and printer locations.
- **Messaging** — chat messages exchanged between users and printer owners about an order.
- **Device** — Firebase Cloud Messaging token for push notifications.

## 3. Purpose & Legal Basis

We process this data to operate the ordering, scheduling, and messaging service you signed up for (Art. 6 (1)(b) GDPR — performance of a contract). Push notifications and security gating rely on our legitimate interest in a usable, abuse-resistant service (Art. 6 (1)(f) GDPR).

## 4. Storage & Security

Data is stored in **Google Firebase** (Cloud Firestore and Storage). Access is restricted by deny-by-default Firestore security rules and a role-based permission model; new accounts remain sandboxed until explicitly granted access. Auth tokens are held in the device secure storage. Cloud Functions run in the `europe-west3` (Frankfurt) region.

## 5. Retention

We retain order, reservation, and message data for as long as your account is active. On account deletion, associated personal data and uploaded files are removed.

## 6. Contact

Privacy enquiries: **salih.oez@proton.me**.
gdprInstructionsMarkdown:
# GDPR Data-Subject Instructions — INT.PrintIt

Your account details, uploaded 3D model files, scheduling data, and chat messages are personal data under the GDPR. You have the following rights over them.

## Your Rights

- **Access (Art. 15)** — request a copy of the data we hold about you.
- **Rectification (Art. 16)** — correct your profile or order details.
- **Erasure (Art. 17)** — request deletion of your account, uploaded files, and messages.
- **Restriction (Art. 18)** — ask us to limit processing while a dispute is resolved.
- **Portability (Art. 20)** — receive your order and message data in a machine-readable format.
- **Object (Art. 21)** — object to processing based on legitimate interest, including push notifications.

## Uploaded Models & Messages

Your uploaded 3D model files are visible only to you, the assigned printer owner, and admins, per our deny-by-default security rules. You can delete an order to remove its files, and chat messages are tied to the order lifecycle. Push notifications can be disabled at any time in your device settings.

## Submitting a Request

Email **salih.oez@proton.me** with subject `GDPR Request — INT.PrintIt`. We respond within **30 days**.

## Complaints

You may lodge a complaint with the State Data Protection Commissioner of North Rhine-Westphalia.
